scope.purpose: independent post-idle whole-stage CPU residency and same-window DRAM traffic evidence
not_authorized: performance or adoption claims from this source contract
point CPU samples as a whole-stage residency substitute
process I/O bytes or theoretical DIMM bandwidth as DRAM traffic

immutable_bindings.runner_result_sha256_semantic: SHA-256 of the immutable summary.json emitted by run_post_idle_benchmark.jl
immutable_bindings.raw_qpc_artifact_sha256_semantic: SHA-256 of the immutable records.jsonl named and hashed by summary.json
immutable_bindings.rule: every producer artifact repeats every digest exactly; the independent producer may not rewrite any bound artifact

etw.stage_interval_rule: begin_qpc and end_qpc must exactly equal the bound records.jsonl raw interval; transformed or rounded timestamps are rejected
etw.owner_rule: validator hashes and parses the exact role-manifest bytes; each stage owner set must equal all manifest registrations for that role, and TIDs, registration QPCs, allowed CPU Sets and per-thread assignment/readback hashes remain pre-bound, so selecting a favorable subset after capture is forbidden
etw.coverage_rule: reconstruct every scheduled slice intersecting the complete stage, including the carried-in thread state before the first in-interval CSwitch and the carried-out state after the final in-interval CSwitch
etw.processor_mapping_rule: each CSwitch processor group/logical index maps to exactly one record in the bound GetSystemCpuSetInformation topology; CPU Set ID, core index and efficiency class must agree
fail_closed_conditions: missing, duplicate or unexpected stage key
trace does not enclose every requested QPC interval
QPC frequency mismatch
any lost ETW event or buffer
circular overwrite, decode failure or unknown CSwitch event version
missing process/thread lifetime or boundary state
scheduled benchmark thread absent from the pre-bound role manifest
unattributed scheduled ticks
scheduled slice on a disallowed role CPU Set
processor-group or topology mapping ambiguity
same-thread reconstructed slice overlap
owner thread has no reconstructed scheduled slice

provider.name: Intel Processor Counter Monitor (Intel PCM)
provider.windows_requirement: Intel PCM Windows driver loaded and bound by SHA-256

imc.availability_statement: stock pcm-memory CSV is not sufficient for this gate because it does not itself bind raw before/after values, counter width, reset, overflow, multiplex and enabled/running time for every populated IMC channel
imc.provenance_binding: capture must equal externally expected SHA-256 values for the availability probe, PCM tool, Windows driver, raw adapter, role manifest, hook source, hook witness, both raw snapshots, counter inventory and counter configuration, plus the exact program generation
imc.timed_region_binding: hashed role manifest and timed-region hook, before/after raw snapshot hashes, counter inventory/configuration hashes, program generation, and QPC snapshot brackets enclosing the exact timed interval
channel_requirements: canonical decimal-string raw before/after and delta counts
QPC time_enabled_ticks equals time_running_ticks equals exact capture duration

aggregate_recomputation.bytes_per_second: bytes*qpc_frequency/duration_qpc_ticks rounded half-up to the nearest integer

imc.counter_guard_limit: pre/post counter-latch guard ticks combined must be at most 0.1 percent of the exact timed region and are reported separately; larger windows fail closed
fail_closed_conditions: raw PCM adapter absent or capability probe fails
Intel PCM tool or Windows driver absent
missing populated channel
counter value exceeds declared width
after is less than before; modulo wrap inference is forbidden
overflow, reset, multiplex, lost sample or unequal enabled/running time
unattributed counter or interfering activity in the system-wide window
QPC frequency or exact capture interval mismatch
aggregate bytes or bandwidth does not reproduce from raw counts
process I/O or theoretical bandwidth was used

etw_checks: xperf.exe file identity, version and SHA-256
xperf -providers kf exposes PROC_THREAD and CSWITCH
imc_checks: pcm-memory.exe file identity, version and SHA-256
Intel PCM raw adapter emits INTEL_PCM_RAW_IMC_RD_WR_COUNTERS_V1 probe JSON
probe JSON declares raw before/after, width, overflow, multiplex, QPC window, read/write IMC and loaded driver support

runner_hook.process_identity: run nonce plus PID plus runner identity QPC; ETW must add and validate the earlier ETW process-start QPC
runner_hook.thread_identity: OS TID and role-registration QPC are hashed before timed work; ETW must add thread-start QPC and prove start <= registration <= stage
runner_hook.counter_handshake: a probed Intel PCM raw adapter must complete a before snapshot before timed_begin_qpc and begin an after snapshot after timed_end_qpc
runner_hook.configuration_rule: before and after acknowledgements must have identical counter inventory SHA-256, configuration SHA-256 and program generation
runner_hook.missing_adapter_behavior: there is no no-op controller; the timed counter handshake throws and the probe emits UNAVAILABLE_FAIL_CLOSED
